entry.title: #SITREC2026 Ten Years of Community, Innovation, and People
entry.content:
On May 23, 2026, the SAP Community gathered once again in Recife for a very special edition of SAP Inside Track. This time, however, the event carried a different meaning. It was not simply another conference or another day filled with technical sessions. It was the celebration of a journey that started ten years ago with a simple idea shared by a group of SAP professionals who believed that knowledge becomes more valuable when it is shared.

Looking around the auditorium that morning, it was impossible not to compare the atmosphere with our first edition back in 2016. What was once a local initiative has grown into one of the most traditional SAP community events in Brazil, bringing together consultants, customers, partners, students and technology leaders who voluntarily dedicate their time to learn from each other.

 The venue was full long before the first presentation started. More than 450 professionals registered for the event, and between 300 and 350 participants joined us throughout the day. The colorful visual identity created especially for the ten-year celebration, the large LED stage, the tropical SAP capybaras decorating the auditorium, the networking areas, coffee breaks, lunch and live streaming all contributed to an environment that felt professional while still preserving the informal spirit that makes every SAP Inside Track unique.

What impressed me the most was seeing experienced consultants discussing architecture with students attending their first SAP event, customers exchanging experiences with partners, and people meeting in person after years of interacting only through SAP Community blogs and LinkedIn. That is exactly what SAP Inside Track has always represented.

Learning Together Is Still the Best Innovation Strategy

 If there was one message that connected every presentation throughout the day, it was that the SAP ecosystem is entering a new era.

The journey started with Antonio Figueredo @afigueredo54  sharing a real customer experience upgrading to SAP S/4HANA 2025. Instead of focusing only on technical aspects, the discussion explored the business motivations behind modernization projects and how SAP Joule and Artificial Intelligence are becoming part of everyday enterprise operations.

From there, the audience moved naturally into Manuel Robalinho's session @MRobalinho , which demonstrated that digital transformation is not only about new applications but also about connecting financial processes, banking services and business ecosystems in a way that makes organizations more efficient and intelligent.

Artificial Intelligence remained one of the dominant themes throughout the event. Leandro Dante challenged the audience with a provocative question that quickly became one of the most discussed topics of the day. Will Clean Core eliminate custom developments, or will AI redefine how extensions are created? Instead of presenting a simple answer, the session encouraged everyone to rethink how SAP solutions will evolve over the next decade.

Thiago Verta Eiras continued this conversation from a functional perspective, reminding us that Business AI does not begin with algorithms. It begins with business processes, governance and well-structured information. This message resonated with many functional consultants who increasingly see themselves as transformation advisors rather than system configurators.

Community itself also became a technical topic. During the SAP Community and SAP Inside Track session, @Marssel700 reflected on how blogs, local events and volunteer initiatives have shaped thousands of SAP careers around the world. Technology evolves rapidly, but communities remain relevant because people continue sharing experiences and helping each other.

The afternoon sessions expanded this vision even further. Discussions about SAP Signavio demonstrated how process intelligence can reduce knowledge gaps in transformation projects. Sessions about BTM showed how business transformation methodologies can accelerate S/4HANA migrations and support Brazilian Tax Reform initiatives. Digital Maintenance presented practical examples of intelligent asset management, while presentations on Low-Code platforms, AI agents and the future of Fiori illustrated how development itself is being reinvented.

One of the most engaging moments happened during the presentation "SAP Consultant – Extinction or Evolution?". The room quickly transformed into an open conversation about careers, new skills and the future of consulting in a world increasingly influenced by generative AI. Rather than creating uncertainty, the discussion reinforced an optimistic message. Technology changes constantly, but curiosity, collaboration and business understanding remain the most valuable skills any consultant can develop.

Every presentation delivered a different perspective, yet together they formed a single narrative about the future of SAP. S/4HANA, Business AI, Clean Core, Process Intelligence, Low-Code and Community are no longer isolated concepts. They are part of the same transformation journey.

For readers interested in exploring these topics further, every session has its own dedicated SAP Community blog and recorded presentation, creating a permanent knowledge repository that extends the experience far beyond the event itself.

More Than an Event, a Community Legacy

The most emotional moments of the day were not necessarily the technical ones. Celebrating ten years gave us the opportunity to recognize the people who quietly helped build this community over the last decade.

Roberta Fernandes @RobertaFernande was honored for her remarkable leadership in revitalizing SAP Inside Track events across Brazil after the pandemic and for establishing a level of organization and collaboration that inspired communities in more than fifteen cities.

Antonio Figueredo @afigueredo54 received recognition for being present since the earliest editions, always contributing as a speaker, volunteer and community supporter. Hélder Lins was celebrated for his continuous support of technology initiatives in Pernambuco and for strengthening connections between local communities through GGTI.

We also recognized Accenture for supporting SAP Inside Track Recife throughout its history and SAP itself for continuously encouraging community initiatives, bringing specialists, innovation and technical content to local events across Brazil.

One of my favorite moments was honoring the organizing team. Anyone who has ever organized a community event knows that dozens of volunteers work behind the scenes for months, coordinating speakers, sponsors, logistics, visual identity, streaming, registrations and countless details that participants rarely notice. Seeing this team receive public recognition was a reminder that communities are built through volunteer work and genuine passion.

The celebration continued with SAP Press book giveaways, the now famous tropical SAP capybaras finding new owners, countless selfies, hundreds of conversations during coffee breaks and a Happy Hour that extended the networking long after the official agenda had ended.Looking back, what makes SAP Inside Track Recife special is not the stage, the LED screen or even the presentations themselves. It is the feeling that everyone who attends becomes part of something larger than a conference.

Ten years ago we started with an idea.

Today we celebrate a community.

And after spending an entire day surrounded by professionals eager to learn, teach and help each other, I am convinced that the next ten years will be even more exciting than the first.

See you at the next SAP Inside Track Recife!

Event Photos

Recorded Presentations #SITREC2026

Previous editions: 2026 2025 2024 2020 2017 2016

entry.title: Re: Filtering Out Conditions in SAC Crosstab Total
entry.content: Luckily, I figured out a way to make this work and still use the automatic total function within the table.  I created a calculated dimension for Pack Code.  I called it "PC".  It was using a simple IF/THEN statement that said it either had a pack code or not.  For instance, any pack code that was not "0" was a given a value of "P" and all others was "N" for not having a pack code.  Then, I put this dimension in the table to the right of the original dimension I was doing the totals.  At this point, I did the Totals on the new "PC" dimension, so all the pack code groupings were given their own total (separate from the non packing code which automatically sorted to the bottom).  So, all the "12's" would be grouped together at the top with their own aggregated total and the "0's" would be on the bottom.

entry.title: Using the AI Adapter in SAP Integration Suite to Generate Integration Incident Summaries
entry.content:
Introduction

When an integration fails, support teams often need to interpret technical error messages before they can understand the root cause and decide on the next action. For experienced integration developers, this may be straightforward, but for first-line support or business users, the message can be difficult to translate into something actionable.

In this mini-blog, a simple proof of concept shows how SAP Integration Suite and a Large Language Model (LLM) can transform a raw integration error payload into a structured incident summary by using the built-in AI Adapter in Cloud Integration.

For adapter capabilities, configuration details, and supported variants, refer to SAP Help and existing other SAP blog post by Shashank_K.

Scenario

In real integration scenarios, an incident often contains only a few technical fields, such as:

  • MPL ID.
  • Timestamp.
  • Endpoint.
  • Error message.

Instead of manually interpreting these values every time, the flow sends the incident details to an LLM and asks it to generate a structured summary containing:

  • Incident summary.
  • Root cause.
  • Severity.
  • Recommendation.

This helps reduce analysis time and makes incident triage easier for support teams.

Solution Overview

The integration flow receives the incident payload through an HTTP endpoint and forwards the content to an LLM using the AI Adapter in SAP Integration Suite. The AI Adapter for SAP Integration Suite facilitates and accelerates connectivity to Generative AI Hub (SAP AI Core) and other AI providers and is available in AICore-GenAIHub and Custom-OpenAI-REST variants. The LLM returns a structured incident summary that can be displayed, logged, or forwarded to another support process.

Client -> Cloud Integration -> AI Adapter -> LLM (SAP AI Core - GenAI Hub) -> Structured Incident Summary

Building the Integration Flow

Prerequisites:
This mini-blog assumes you are familiar with creating integration flows in Cloud Integration of SAP Integration Suite and have already provisioned SAP AI Core with GenAI Hub enabled. If you are new to SAP AI Core, you can start with the SAP Developers mission to set up SAP AI Core on SAP Business Technology Platform and then explore concepts such as orchestration and the foundation models that make up SAP AI Core: Set Up SAP AI Core in SAP BTP.

The Integration Flow in my demo scenario contains the following steps:

  • HTTPS Sender Adapter.
  • JSON to XML Converter.
  • Content Modifier.
  • Request-Reply step.
  • AI Adapter.
  • JSON to XML Converter.
  • Content Modifier.
  • HTTPS Response.

Here is how my Integration Flow looks like. The JSON to XML Converter step transforms the incoming JSON payload into XML format so it can be processed more easily by downstream steps in the iFlow, since the payload is sent as JSON from Bruno, a REST client tool.

Create the exchange properties below to map the values that will be used later in the message flow between Request Reply 1 and the Receiver.

On Message Flow between Request Reply 1 to Receiver, fill-in the connection details (you can get the details from SAP AI Core > Service Key).

Before filling the OAuth2 Client Credentials field, first create the OAuth2 Client Credentials in Monitor > Integrations and APIs > Manage Security Material. Here are example configurations.

In my demo scenario, for the Processing details configuration, I'm using the following:
 

Operation

Orchestration - Completion

Orchestration - Deployment ID

$header.deployment_id

AI Resource Group

Default

Request Payload Source

UI Configurable

Model

Gemini-2.5-flash

Role

Assistant

Input

You are an SAP Integration Suite support assistant. Analyze ONLY the provided incident information (Timestamp=${property.timestamp}, Endpoint=${property.endpoint}, Error Message=${property.errorMessage}) and return ONLY: Summary, Root Cause, Severity (Low|Medium|High), Recommendation. Max 100 words. No explanations, no markdown, no placeholders, no extra text.

Request Headers

*

As I don't want to hard-code the Orchestration - Deployment ID, so I'm passing it in my HTTP headers with key name deployment_id.

Refer to this SAP Help documentation page, if you need to do variant of configuration such as using Exchange Body as Request Payload Source allows you to specify required structure and values.
Note: the input content should be 1 liner, otherwise you will get an error upon deployment.

Map the result into aiResult in Exchange Property:

Finally in Message Body return the following JSON as final output:

Testing the Flow

You can test the flow using Bruno or any REST client tool (i.e. Postman or cUrl) by sending the incident payload to the HTTP endpoint.

Input payload:

{
	  "mplId": "AGo046SjNI9VZ4l",
	  "timestamp": "2026-06-22T10:00:00Z",
	  "endpoint": "/api/orders",
	  "errorMessage": "OAuth token validation failed"
}

Response payload:
 

{
	"incidentAnalysis": "Summary: API calls to /api/orders are failing due to OAuth token validation failure.
	Root Cause: Invalid or expired OAuth token used for authentication to the /api/orders endpoint.
	Severity: Medium
	Recommendation: Verify current OAuth token validity, expiration, and scopes. Regenerate or refresh the token as needed."
}

Example from Bruno:

Key Takeaways

SAP Integration Suite can do more than move data between systems — with the AI Adapter, it becomes easier to integrate with an LLM and turn raw technical errors into structured summaries. This helps support teams understand failures faster, identify likely root causes, and take the right next action.

entry.title: Going Beyond the Tip of the Iceberg with SAP HANA Cloud SQL on Files
entry.content:
Related Blogs:
  • Unlocking the True Potential of Data in Files with SAP HANA Database SQL on Files in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Exploring New SQL on Files Use Cases with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024 and QRC 01/2025
  • Accelerating Your Analytics with Delta Table Replication into SAP HANA Cloud

As I mentioned in my previous blogs, SQL on Files, a capability of SAP HANA database in SAP HANA Cloud, has been continuously expanding since SAP HANA Cloud QRC 3/2024. We started with native read-only access to CSV, Parquet, and Delta tables in SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Files. We then extended this access to external object storages (Amazon S3, Azure Blob/ADLS Gen2, Google Cloud Storage) and external Delta Sharing in QRC 4/2024. Most recently, we introduced Delta table-to-HANA replication in QRC 4/2025.

With SAP HANA Cloud QRC 2/2026, I'm pleased to announce that the journey continues, and this time the spotlight is on Apache Iceberg, the industry-standard open table format that has rapidly become a cornerstone of modern data lakehouse architectures. While Iceberg support has been quietly maturing in SAP HANA Cloud over the last few quarters, this is the first blog dedicated to telling the full story end-to-end.

In a nutshell, here is what SAP HANA Cloud now offers for Apache Iceberg, cumulatively from QRC 3/2025 through QRC 2/2026:

  • Direct read-only access to Apache Iceberg tables in object storage via the file adapter, including time-travel queries (QRC 3/2025).
  • Snapshot replica toggling support for Apache Iceberg virtual tables (QRC 1/2026).
  • Data Page V2 (DATA_PAGE_V2) support in the Parquet reader, aligning SAP HANA Cloud with the latest Parquet ecosystem (QRC 1/2026).
  • Direct read-only access to Apache Iceberg REST Catalogs via the new icebergcatalog adapter. QRC 1/2026 introduced the adapter with Cloudera and Databricks running on AWS as the initially certified providers, and QRC 2/2026 extends the multi-cloud coverage to Azure and GCP for Databricks while additionally certifying Snowflake across AWS, Azure, and GCP. Cloudera certification on Azure and GCP is planned for a future release.
  • AliCloud Object Storage Service (OSS) added to the list of supported external object storages (QRC 2/2026).

Alright, with these points in mind, let's dive deeper into the details with some examples.

 

Apache Iceberg via the file Adapter

Starting with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 3/2025, SQL on Files began supporting Apache Iceberg as an additional file format on top of the existing CSV, Parquet, and Delta table support. As highlighted in my first SQL on Files blog and follow-up blog, the file adapter provides direct read-only access to files in SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Files or in supported external object storages. With Iceberg now in the mix, customers can query Iceberg tables sitting on object storage without any data movement or ingestion.

Since Apache Iceberg is an open table format, the way of creating a virtual table is similar to Delta tables, where you only need to point the virtual table to the root of the Iceberg table:

-- create a virtual table by pointing to an apache iceberg table
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE DEMO.ICEBERG_ORDERS AT "demo-hdlf_rs"."/iceberg/orders/" AS ICEBERG;

A few things worth knowing about this support:

  • Both Iceberg v1 (Copy-on-Write) and v2 (Merge-on-Read) are supported for read-only access.
  • Time-travel queries are supported, similar to Delta tables, via either snapshot ID or timestamp:
-- time travel by snapshot id
SELECT * FROM DEMO.ICEBERG_ORDERS FOR VERSION AS OF '2953155114686890267';

-- time travel by timestamp
SELECT * FROM DEMO.ICEBERG_ORDERS FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF '2025-04-08 07:43:31.793000000';
  • Two new built-in functions help you explore Iceberg metadata directly from SAP HANA Cloud:
FunctionReturns
GET_ICEBERG_TABLE_SNAPSHOTSSnapshot IDs, create time, sequence number, record count, file count, total file size
GET_VIRTUAL_TABLE_FILE_PARTITIONSPartition values, file count, total file size per partition
  • The existing GET_REMOTE_SOURCE_FILE_COLUMNS built-in procedure has been extended to retrieve column information from Iceberg tables as well.

Important: Apache Iceberg metadata stores absolute table paths. Simply copying Iceberg files from one location to another will cause inconsistencies, and creating virtual tables on the copied files will not work. Always create or migrate Iceberg tables using a proper Iceberg-compatible engine.

Please refer to the links below for further details.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Create a Virtual Table
  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: GET_ICEBERG_TABLE_SNAPSHOTS Function
  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: GET_VIRTUAL_TABLE_FILE_PARTITIONS Function
  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: GET_REMOTE_SOURCE_FILE_COLUMNS

 

Apache Iceberg REST Catalog via the icebergcatalog Adapter

Reading Iceberg tables directly from object storage with the file adapter is great for self-managed scenarios. However, in real-world enterprise lakehouses, Iceberg tables are usually managed by an Iceberg REST Catalog server, for example, Databricks Unity Catalog, Cloudera Data Platform, or Snowflake. Customers told us that, for production-grade integration, the catalog-based approach is what they consider enterprise-ready: it centralizes table management, access control, and metadata governance.

That's where the new icebergcatalog adapter comes in. Starting with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 1/2026, SQL on Files supports direct read-only access to external Apache Iceberg REST Catalogs, with Cloudera and Databricks running on AWS as the initially certified providers. With QRC 2/2026, the multi-cloud coverage is extended to Azure (ADLS Gen2) and GCP (GCS) for Databricks, and Snowflake is added as an additionally certified provider across AWS, Azure, and GCP. Cloudera certification on Azure and GCP is planned for a future release.

The way to create a remote source to an Iceberg REST Catalog follows the official Apache Iceberg REST Catalog specification with bearer-token (OAuth) authentication:

-- create a remote source to an iceberg rest catalog
CREATE REMOTE SOURCE <remote_source_name> ADAPTER "icebergcatalog" CONFIGURATION '
provider=databricks;
endpoint=<rest_endpoint>;'
WITH CREDENTIAL TYPE 'OAUTH' USING 'access_token=<bearer_token>';

Once the remote source is created, virtual tables are created in the same way as with other SAP HANA smart data access (a.k.a., SDA) adapters by pointing to the catalog → namespace → table:

-- create a virtual table on a table exposed via the iceberg rest catalog
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE DEMO.VT_ORDERS
  AT "<remote_source_name>"."<NULL>"."<namespace>"."<table>";

A few important notes on the certification scope and authentication:

  • The icebergcatalog adapter is designed to work with any spec-compliant Apache Iceberg REST Catalog. In theory, any REST catalog that follows the official spec and bearer-token authentication should work.
  • However, because small differences in authentication flows or vendor-specific extensions can cause unexpected issues, SAP officially certifies only a defined set of Iceberg REST Catalog providers per QRC, as summarized below. Other REST catalogs may work but are not officially certified.
ProviderAWSAzure (ADLS Gen2)GCP (GCS)
ClouderaCertified (QRC 1/2026)PlannedPlanned
DatabricksCertified (QRC 1/2026)Certified (QRC 2/2026)Certified (QRC 2/2026)
SnowflakeCertified (QRC 2/2026)Certified (QRC 2/2026)Certified (QRC 2/2026)
  • Authentication is currently limited to bearer-token (OAuth). Full credential flows (e.g., automatic token acquisition and refresh by negotiating directly with the identity provider) are not implemented inside SAP HANA Cloud. Instead, the credential flow is expected to live in the application layer, while the database layer simply consumes the access token.

For renewing access tokens, the SET SESSION CREDENTIAL statement can be used to inject a freshly obtained access token from the application layer:

-- renew the access token from the application layer
SET SESSION CREDENTIAL FOR REMOTE SOURCE <remote_source_name>
  TYPE 'OAUTH' USING 'access_token=<access_token_string>';

In other words, our expectation is that the credential flows are implemented in the application layer, while the database layer supports the way of renewing access tokens.

Please refer to the links below for further details.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Create an Apache Iceberg Catalog Remote Source

 

More Improvements Worth Highlighting

Beyond the two main pillars above, several smaller but meaningful improvements landed alongside this Iceberg journey. They are bundled here for completeness.

Snapshot replica toggling for Apache Iceberg (QRC 1/2026)

For customers who want better query performance on Iceberg tables, snapshot replica toggling is now supported when the source is an Apache Iceberg table. As with other SDA-based virtual tables, you can toggle a virtual table to a snapshot replica by adding a snapshot replica with the ALTER VIRTUAL TABLE statement, then refresh or drop it as needed:

-- toggle to a snapshot replica
ALTER VIRTUAL TABLE DEMO.VT_ORDERS ADD SHARED SNAPSHOT REPLICA;

-- refresh the snapshot replica with the latest data from the source
ALTER VIRTUAL TABLE DEMO.VT_ORDERS REFRESH SNAPSHOT REPLICA;

-- delete the replica
ALTER VIRTUAL TABLE DEMO.VT_ORDERS DROP REPLICA;

A couple of important constraints to keep in mind:

  • Snapshot replica toggling always creates a full snapshot of the data the virtual table is pointing to. This means it does not help if the target Iceberg table holds a vast volume of data that cannot be replicated in full into SAP HANA Cloud. In such cases, the recommended approach is to keep federation only, or wait for the upcoming chunk-based replication discussed in Looking Ahead.
  • Unlike Delta tables, real-time replication via toggling is not yet supported for Iceberg. Real-time replication on Iceberg will become available together with the upcoming interval-based CDC support (see Looking Ahead).

Data Page V2 support in the Parquet reader (QRC 1/2026)

The Parquet ecosystem has been moving toward Data Page V2 (page_type=DATA_PAGE_V2), and several modern engines, now produce Parquet files using this newer page format. Starting with QRC 1/2026, the SAP HANA Cloud Parquet reader supports DATA_PAGE_V2, ensuring smooth interoperability with these engines. This is a quiet but important enabler for the full Iceberg story, especially for the Apache Iceberg REST Catalog scenario with Snowflake-managed Iceberg.

AliCloud Object Storage Service (OSS) support (QRC 2/2026)

The list of supported external object storages keeps growing. With QRC 2/2026, AliCloud Object Storage Service (OSS) joins Amazon S3, Azure Blob/ADLS Gen2, and Google Cloud Storage as a first-class storage option for SQL on Files.

However, please note one limitation specific to AliCloud deployments:

  • SQL on Files queries accessing Google Cloud Storage on AliCloud Cloud deployments are not supported.

Please refer to the links below for further details.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Toggle Between Virtual Tables and Replica Tables

 

FAQs

Q: Can I write to an Apache Iceberg table from SQL on Files?
A: No. SQL on Files is read-only by design, both via the file adapter and the icebergcatalog adapter. INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE on virtual tables pointing to Iceberg are not supported. Write operations should be performed by Iceberg-native engines (e.g., Spark, the catalog provider's compute) on the source side.

Q: Which Iceberg REST Catalog providers are officially certified?
A: Cloudera and Databricks running on AWS were initially certified with QRC 1/2026. With QRC 2/2026, Databricks is additionally certified on Azure and GCP, and Snowflake is newly certified across AWS, Azure, and GCP. Cloudera certification on Azure and GCP is planned for a future release. See the certification table in the icebergcatalog adapter section above for the full per-QRC, per-hyperscaler matrix. Other spec-compliant REST catalogs may work but are not officially certified.

Q: What kind of authentication is supported for the icebergcatalog adapter?
A: Bearer-token (OAuth) only. Full credential flows are expected to be handled in the application layer, with the access token being injected into SAP HANA Cloud either at remote source creation time or via SET SESSION CREDENTIAL for refresh.

Q: Can I replicate an Apache Iceberg table into a SAP HANA table?
A: Today, only snapshot replica toggling is supported for Iceberg. Full remote subscription-based replication (initial load + interval-based CDC), equivalent to the Delta table replication introduced in QRC 4/2025, is on the roadmap (see Looking Ahead).

Q: I'm running Google Cloud Storage on AliCloud. Can I use SQL on Files to read data from there?
A: No. SQL on Files queries accessing Google Cloud Storage are not supported on AliCloud deployments. Other supported object storages remain accessible.

Q: Is there any plan to support Apache Iceberg format V3?
A: The Iceberg ecosystem is evolving toward V3, and we are actively defining our adoption strategy. See Looking Ahead for the high-level direction.

Q: I'm already accessing Apache Iceberg tables via the file adapter. Do I need to change anything with the introduction of the icebergcatalog adapter?
A: No. The two adapters cover different scenarios and coexist. If you have been using the file adapter to access Iceberg tables directly on object storage, your existing setup keeps working as-is. The icebergcatalog adapter is an additional option for customers who manage their Iceberg tables through an Iceberg REST Catalog (Cloudera, Databricks, or Snowflake).

 

Looking Ahead

While this blog focuses on what is generally available with QRC 2/2026, there are a couple of directions we are actively exploring for the longer term:

  • Apache Iceberg table-to-HANA replication: Today, Delta table replication (QRC 4/2025) gives customers chunk-based or one-step initial load combined with user-driven or scheduled CDC. We see strong demand for the equivalent capability on Apache Iceberg via both the file adapter and the icebergcatalog adapter, including Data Replication UI integration. Release timing has not yet been finalized.
  • Apache Iceberg format V3: V3 introduces extended types (e.g., variant, geometry, geography, nanosecond timestamps), default values, row lineage, and binary deletion vectors. We plan to selectively enable V3 capabilities as the broader ecosystem matures, so customers get predictable behavior with V3 tables. Release timing has not yet been finalized.

These items reflect long-term direction rather than committed deliveries, so please treat them as forward-looking signals rather than firm release plans.

 

Conclusion

With SAP HANA Cloud QRC 2/2026, the Apache Iceberg story in SQL on Files is now complete in its first chapter: direct read-only access to Iceberg tables via the file adapter, enterprise-grade integration with Iceberg REST Catalogs via the new icebergcatalog adapter across AWS, Azure, and GCP, plus a set of supporting improvements like snapshot replica toggling, Data Page V2, and AliCloud OSS coverage.

This continues SAP HANA Cloud's commitment to open table formats, meeting customers where their data already lives, whether that's a Delta Lake, an Iceberg lakehouse, or a mixture of both. As we remain committed to innovation, stay tuned for upcoming updates that will continue to expand and enrich your SAP HANA Cloud experience, including richer Iceberg replication and evolving Iceberg V3 support.

entry.content:
Inspection Type 04 with HU is active AND Inspection Type 08 + 09 are also active.

Show me error when I do Good Receipt : Change the inspection stock of material 0062 in QM only.

When I remove inspection type 08 and 09 from material master system allows me to do good receipt from production order; movement type 101.

which parameter I I need to include in customizing or material master to allow me works with inspection type 04 with Handling unit, Inspection Type 08 and 09 active in material master.

entry.title: 在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 中通过自定义逻辑在创建外向交货单时实现库存数量不足提醒
entry.content:
问题背景

有客户提出需求，由于业务管理要求,希望在创建外向交货单时能够自动检查交货单行项目物料的库存数量是否充足。具体来说，当外向交货单创建保存时，需要检查交货单对应的销售订单行项目物料数量是否大于物料库存数量，如果库存数量不足，则增加一个 Warning 提示该物料库存数量不足。同时，客户希望在使用 API 创建外向交货单时，也能看到对应的 Message。

解决方案

尝试通过自定义逻辑,使用两个 BAdI 的组合方案来满足需求。

分析步骤

1. 确认适用的 BAdI 增强点

首先,我们需要找到合适的 BAdI 增强点来实现库存检查功能。经过分析,发现增强点 LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK 可以在交货单保存之前执行最终检查。因此,可以通过这个 BAdI 实现在前台 APP 创建外向交货单时，如果库存数量不足，返回 Warning 消息提醒。

2. 实现前台 APP 的 Warning 提醒

在 BAdI LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK 中实现基本的库存检查逻辑：

    CONSTANTS:
    lc_plant    TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
    lc_vkorg    TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
    lc_vtweg    TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
    lc_spart    TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

    DATA:
    lv_so_qty    TYPE menge_d,
    lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

    IF documentprocessingmode = 'CREATE'.
      LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item) .
      IF ls_item-plant = lc_plant.
      " 读取销售订单行项目的订单数量
      SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
      FROM i_salesorderitem
      WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
        AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
      INTO @LV_so_qty.
      " 读取物料的库存数量
      SELECT SINGLE MATLWRHSSTKQTYINMATLBASEUNIT
      FROM I_STOCKQUANTITYCURRENTVALUE_2( P_DisplayCurrency = 'CNY' )
      WHERE PRODUCT = @LS_item-MATERIAL
      INTO @LV_stock.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        CONTINUE. " No SO item found – skip this delivery item
      ENDIF.
      IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
        message-messagetype = 'W'. " saving of the delivery document will be warned
        message-messagetext = |Warning: 该物料库存数量不足|.
        EXIT.
      ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

3. 发现 API 场景的局限性

但是这里的 Warning Message 只能在前台 APP 显示，API 创建的情况下无法看到 Message。因此需要通过替代方案实现。

4. 实现 API 场景的消息提示

为了让 API 创建场景也能看到提示信息，我们采用以下方案：

方案说明：

- 创建一个自定义字段，这个字段可以在 API Response 里看到。我们创建了一个自定义字段
- 使用 BAdI LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE，这个 BAdI 可以修改表头和行项目级别的标准字段和自定义字段
- 该 BAdI 将在交货单保存到数据库之前被调用

实现代码：

IF DOCUMENTPROCESSINGMODE = 'CREATE'.
CONSTANTS:
  lc_plant  TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
  lc_vkorg  TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
  lc_vtweg  TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
  lc_spart  TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

DATA:
  lv_so_qty TYPE menge_d,
  lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

" -------------------------------------------------------
" Step 1: 必须先将输入完整复制到输出（Cloud BAdI 标准做法）
" -------------------------------------------------------
move-corresponding delivery_document_in to delivery_document_out.
delivery_document_items_out = delivery_document_items_in.

" -------------------------------------------------------
" Step 2: 表头组织数据过滤
" 仅对 Sales Org 1310 / DC 20 / Division 00 生效
" -------------------------------------------------------
*CHECK delivery_document_in-salesorganization = lc_vkorg.

" -------------------------------------------------------
" Step 3: 遍历交货单行项目
" -------------------------------------------------------
LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item).


  " 跳过无参考销售订单的行项目
*  CHECK ls_item-referencesddocument IS NOT INITIAL.
*  CHECK ls_item-referencesddocumentitem IS NOT INITIAL.

  " 读取销售订单行项目的订单数量
  SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
    FROM i_salesorderitem
    WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
      AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
    INTO @LV_so_qty.
  " 读取物料的库存数量
  SELECT SINGLE MATLWRHSSTKQTYINMATLBASEUNIT
    FROM I_STOCKQUANTITYCURRENTVALUE_2( P_DisplayCurrency = 'CNY' )
    WHERE PRODUCT = @LS_item-MATERIAL
    INTO @LV_stock.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    CONTINUE. " 未找到对应销售订单行项目，跳过
  ENDIF.

  " 比较库存数量与销售订单数量
  IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
    " 更新自定义字段
    DELIVERY_DOCUMENT_OUT-yy1_message_dlh = 'Warning: 该物料库存数量不足。'.
  ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

5. 使用到的 CDS View

在实现过程中,我们使用了以下 CDS View 来获取所需的数据:

- I_SalesOrderItem: 用于获取销售订单行项目的订单数量,通过销售订单号和行项目号进行查询
- I_StockQuantityCurrentValue_2: 用于获取物料的当前库存数量,提供了物料在不同存储地点的实时库存信息,非常适合用于库存检查场景

6. 测试验证

测试后，上述代码可以满足需求:

- 在前台 APP 创建外向交货单时，如果库存不足，会显示 Warning 消息

 

- 通过 API 创建外向交货单时，自定义字段的值被更改，可以在 Response 的自定义字段中看到库存不足的提示信息

 

结语

通过阅读本文以及之前写过的几篇相似场景的 Blog，希望您已知晓在创建外向交货单时，如何通过自定义逻辑来实现库存数量检查并在不同场景下返回相应的提示消息。

关注以下链接可以看到更多有关 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 销售相关的问题和博文：SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales - SAP Community

如果您对本文有任何的建议和想法,欢迎在评论区提出,期待与您一起交流。也欢迎您关注我的个人账号，期待我们在下一篇博文再见！

entry.title: Two Ways to Build a Hierarchy in SAC and How to Choose the Right One
entry.content:
If you have ever built a SAC story showing regional sales or cost centre spending, you have used a hierarchy even if you did not think of it that way. Hierarchies let a table start at 'Global' and let users click down through continent, country, and city without leaving the page.

SAC supports two hierarchy types: level-based and parent-child. They look similar in a story but work differently, are set up differently, and suit different source data. Choosing the wrong one leads to blank values, missing nodes, or a hierarchy that simply does not behave. This post explains both types, how to set them up, how to use them in stories, and how to pick the right one.

1. The Two Hierarchy Types - What Makes Them Different

A level-based hierarchy has one column per level in your source table. A three-level geography hierarchy needs three columns: continent, country, city. Every branch goes exactly as deep as the number of level columns you define, the depth is fixed and uniform across the whole tree.

A parent-child hierarchy uses just two key columns: a node ID and a parent ID. Each row says "This node belongs to this parent". SAC reads those relationships and builds the tree automatically - depth can vary branch by branch. This is the native format of ERP and financial systems like SAP BW, BPC, and S/4HANA.

Key takeaway: Level-based = one column per level, uniform depth. Parent-child = two columns, any depth, ideal for ERP data.

                                  

2. Setting Up a Level-Based Hierarchy

First, check your source data has one column per level. For a three-level geography structure that means continent, country, and city columns - each row repeats the parent values for every leaf record.

In SAC Model Builder, open the Datasets tab, you will find a level hierarchy as shown below in image, then drag your level columns into order from Level 0 (most general) down to the most granular. Give it a clear name, save, and deploy. The hierarchy then appears as a drill-down option on any table or chart using that dimension. There are two ways to do this:

        

                        

Practical tip: use short descriptive names for level columns such as  'L0 — Continent', 'L1 — Country', 'L2 — City' rather than generic labels like 'Column A'. It makes the model far easier to maintain.

Key takeaway: One column per level in the source, configure in Model Builder, name and deploy. Available in every story that uses that model.

                   

3. Setting Up a Parent-Child Hierarchy

Your source needs two key columns: a node ID and a parent ID. The root node - the top of the tree, such as 'Global' - has no parent, so its parent field is empty. Every other node points to its immediate parent.

SAC identifies the root, finds all nodes whose parent ID matches it, then recurses down through each level automatically. Depth is driven entirely by the data - SAC does not need to be told how many levels to expect. In Model Builder, configure the dimension to reference the node ID and parent ID columns, then save and deploy.

The most common mistake is inconsistent IDs - a parent ID that does not exactly match any node ID in the table. This causes orphaned nodes that do not appear in the tree. Always validate your IDs before importing.

Key takeaway: Two columns - node ID and parent ID - are all SAC needs. Variable depth is handled automatically. Check ID consistency before importing.

                      

4. Using Hierarchies in Stories

Once a hierarchy is defined in the model, there are four interactions worth knowing in stories:

  • Drill down: Right-click a node and select Drill Down to expand just that member and hide everything else. Useful when a user wants to focus on a single branch.
  • Expand and collapse: Click ▶ on any node to reveal its children while keeping the parent total visible. The most natural way for users to browse — open what you need, leave the rest collapsed.
  • Hierarchy display setting: In table or chart properties, choose between indented (default, shows tree structure), flat list, or custom levels. Indented is best for most audiences.
  • Flattening: Filter by hierarchy level in the filter bar to show only one level at a time — for example, only countries. Gives a clean, focused view without manual collapsing.

Key takeaway: Expand/collapse for browsing, drill down for focus, flattening for presenting one level at a time. Match the interaction to what your audience actually needs.

                                              

            

5. Which Type Should You Use?

The decision is usually straightforward once you look at your source data:

  • Flat table, one column per level, uniform depth → use level-based
  • Data from SAP BW, BPC, S/4HANA, or any ERP system → use parent-child
  • Tree depth varies branch by branch → you must use parent-child
  • Unsure or building from scratch → start with level-based, migrate later if needed

The most common mistake is trying to force uneven-depth data into a level-based hierarchy. If some branches go three levels deep and others go six, level-based will produce empty columns and confusing results. Parent-child is the right tool for that job.

Key takeaway: Match the hierarchy type to your source data structure. When in doubt, level-based is simpler to set up and easier to troubleshoot.

Final Thoughts

A well-configured hierarchy makes a story feel natural and powerful , users explore data at the level they need without multiple separate reports. A badly configured one creates blank values and a loss of trust in the numbers.

The things to remember: know your source data structure before choosing a type, use level-based for flat uniform data and parent-child for ERP or variable-depth data, and learn all four story interactions, each serves a different purpose.

If a hierarchy is misbehaving, the usual culprits are a type mismatch with the source, inconsistent parent IDs, or level columns in the wrong order. Start there and you will find the issue quickly.

Thanks for reading. If hierarchies have given you grief on a real build, I would love to hear about it in the comments.

entry.title: Fields in position object couldn't be replaced with "NULL" value in SuccessFactors RCM/ONB
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am currently supporting an AMS for SuccessFactors RCM, ONB 2.0, and EC. We have run into a technical deadlock regarding data propagation during the Internal Hire (IJP / Crossboarding) process when moving an employee between positions with different Org Layer depths.

The Business Scenario: An internal employee is transferring from:

  • Old Position: Has Org structure down to the Section layer (Department: A -> Section: X).

  • New Position: Org structure stops at the Department layer (Department: B -> Section: NULL / Blank).

The Integration Setup: In Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping, the EC field jobInfo.section is mapped directly to the Job Requisition's Section field (which is legitimately blank/null on the Requisition).

The Symptom: 

in QAS -> When initiating Onboarding for this candidate, the payload fails at the very first step (Validate HRIS data for user) with the following error:

"jobInfo : Section '40000065' is not associated with Department '30000314'. Check the Section data object configuration and enter a valid Department."

in PRD -> Initiate Onboarding were working fine with no issue. Although, after the Actions has been created with expected event reason then value in Section field from previous position still there while it should be "NULL".

Our Investigation so far:

  1. The system correctly passes all fields from the Job Req. However, it refuses to pass the NULL value for the Section in Initiate Onboarding step. Instead, it retains the employee's legacy Section (40000065), which causes an MDF Parent-Child association violation while initiating onboarding.

  2. The configuration for Recruit to Hire Data Mapping and Business Rules are correctly set up.

Our Questions to the Experts:

  1. How is this standard scenario (moving from a "deep" org layer to a "shallow" org layer) intended to be handled in SuccessFactors without breaking MDF associations?

  2. What are workarounds to fix this kind of issue? I have check all configuration I can think about in PRD and QAS are the same.

Any insights, workarounds, or shared battle scars regarding this scenario would be immensely appreciated!

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: Two Ways to Build a Hierarchy in SAC and How to Choose the Right One
entry.content: Great explanation of SAC hierarchies and when to use each type. I especially liked the practical comparison between level-based and parent-child along with the common pitfalls section. Clear, useful, and easy to apply in real projects — thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud - Trail balance/P&L - how to expand all...
entry.content:
Good question. If this is an SAC table/report with hierarchy enabled, you can usually right-click the hierarchy dimension and choose “Expand All” (or use the table toolbar menu if available) instead of expanding node by node.

If the option is not available, another approach is to set the hierarchy display to show all levels by default in the story/table settings. Availability can vary depending on the widget type and data source (Live vs Import model).

Expanding one-by-one becomes difficult for large hierarchies, so enabling default expansion at design time is normally the better approach.

entry.title: Modernizing SAP Commerce Cloud: A Practical Guide to the JDK 21 & Spring 6 Migration
entry.content:
The framework update for SAP Commerce Cloud marks one of the most significant platform modernizations in recent years. At its core, this release upgrades the runtime to Java 21 and the application framework to Spring 6, delivering a more secure, performant, and future-proof foundation for your commerce implementation. This article walks through the key changes, architectural decisions, and migration steps you'll need to navigate a successful upgrade.

Why This Upgrade Matters

Java 21 is a Long-Term Support (LTS) release that brings substantial improvements in performance, memory management, and language features over Java 17. Pairing it with Spring 6 — which requires Jakarta EE 9+ as a baseline — positions SAP Commerce Cloud on a modern, actively maintained stack. Beyond raw performance, this upgrade also streamlines authentication by replacing the previous OAuth2 extension with the Spring Authorization Server framework, and removes several deprecated extension sets that have accumulated technical debt over prior releases.

The key changes at a glance:

  • Java 17 → Java 21 (LTS)
  • Spring 5 → Spring 6
  • OAuth2 extension replaced with the Spring Authorization Server framework
  • Removal of deprecated cockpit-related and Amazon Cloud extensions
  • Upgrade of third-party libraries and dependent components
  • Package namespace migration from javax to jakarta

Cleaning Up: Removed JARs and Deprecated Extensions

Deleted JARs

As part of the Jakarta EE migration, the following legacy JSTL JARs must be removed from your custom storefront's WEB-INF/lib directory:

jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-1.2.6

javax.servlet.jsp.jstl-1.2.5

 jstl-impl-1.2.jar

These are superseded by Jakarta-compatible equivalents bundled with the platform.

Removed Extensions

The oauth2 extension has been fully retired and replaced by three new extensions:

  • authorizationserver
  • resourceserver
  • oauth2commons

Update your localextensions.xml to include entries for all three. Any build configuration or dependency referencing the old oauth2 extension must be updated accordingly.

Build Changes: The javax → jakarta Namespace Migration

Spring 6 mandates Jakarta EE 9+, which means the javax.* package namespace has moved to jakarta.* across the board. This is the most pervasive source of compilation errors during the upgrade.

What to update:

  • All imports referencing javax.servlet.*, javax.persistence.*, javax.validation.*, etc. must be changed to their jakarta.* equivalents.
  • Review custom filters, servlets, and JPA entities — these are the most common touch points.
  • Third-party libraries that have not yet migrated to Jakarta may require version bumps or replacements.

A project-wide search-and-replace is a practical first step, but follow up with a full build validation to catch transitive dependencies that pull in old javax packages.

Authentication Overhaul: OAuth2 → Spring Authorization Server

New OAuth Client Model

The Spring Authorization Server introduces a revised OAuthClientDetailsModel with new attributes. While the creation workflow for OAuth clients remains conceptually similar to the deprecated implementation, teams should audit existing client configurations and map them to the new model's attribute set before migration.

Handling Multi-Site Login Pages

The new OAuth framework enforces a single Login Page URI per OAuth client. For implementations that serve multiple country-specific storefronts — each with its own branded login page — this constraint requires a deliberate architectural response.

The recommended approach: per-country OAuth clients.

Rather than sharing a single OAuth client across all storefronts, create a dedicated OAuth client for each country site, following a naming convention which scales cleanly as new markets are added and keeps authentication configuration explicit and auditable per region.

Assisted Service Module (ASM) Updates

ASM has received dedicated authentication support to align with the new OAuth flow.

What Changed

A new asm-login page has been introduced for each site, and a separate OAuth client is now required specifically for ASM. This separation is necessary because the ASM authentication process operates differently from standard customer-facing flows under the new Spring Authorization Server.

Accessing ASM

The ASM session is initiated via a dedicated URL:

https://{site-url}/asm-login/?asm=true

Login flow:

  1. Navigate to the ASM login URL — the asm-login page loads.
  2. Click the "Login as Agent" link in the page header.
  3. The agent is redirected to the default login page and authenticates with ASAGENT credentials.
  4. On successful authentication, the ASM session is established and full agent functionality is available.

Post-login capabilities for agents:

  • Search for a specific customer account
  • Start an emulated session on behalf of that customer
  • Perform actions and operations as the selected customer within the system

CORS Configuration for New Extensions

The authorization server and resource server extensions introduce new CORS filter properties. Add the following to your configuration:

corsfilter.authorizationserver.allowedOrigins=

corsfilter.authorizationserver.allowedOriginPatterns=

corsfilter.authorizationserver.allowCredentials=

Configure these properties to match the allowed origins for your storefront and API consumers.

Summary

The JDK 21 and Spring 6 upgrade is a foundational modernization that sets SAP Commerce Cloud on the path to Jakarta EE, improved runtime performance, and a more standards-aligned authentication model. The migration effort is non-trivial — the javax to jakarta namespace change alone touches virtually every custom extension — but the architectural improvements, particularly the per-country OAuth client strategy and the cleaner ASM authentication flow, provide a more robust and maintainable platform for future development.

Plan your migration with sufficient lead time, validate thoroughly in a non-production environment, and use a pre- and post-deployment checklist above to reduce deployment risk.

For the latest guidance, refer to the official SAP Commerce Cloud release notes and SAP Help Portal documentation for the 2211 release.

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 18 - RFC for UUIDs
entry.content:
The Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) is the premier standards development organisation for the Internet. One of the ways that their work finds its way to the public in general and to engineers and techninians in particular is via documents, specifically RFCs (RFC stood originally for Requests For Comments). Each one is numbered and they describe fundamentals of the Internet, with RFCs ranging from core building blocks such as TCP/IP:

  • RFC 793 Transmission Control Protocol (TCP)
  • RFC 791 Internet Protocol (IP)

protocols that you and I deal with more closely and regularly, such as:

  • RFC2616 HTTP/1.1

as well as somewhat more "unusual" protocols, such as RFC1149, and even non-protocol defining RFCs such as:

  • RFC2119 Key words for use in RFCs to Indicate Requirement Levels

which is as wonderful as it is useful and meta.

RFCs remain essential to how and why things work.

In CDS modelling there are many built-in types, such as Boolean, Integer, DateTime and more. One of these built-in types is UUID, the type behind the common aspect cuid, which is defined thus:

aspect cuid {
  key ID : UUID;
}

This built-in type UUID is compliant with a specific standard described in an RFC document. What is that RFC document's number?

This is a question from the June Developer Challenge on CAP Knowledge. And don't forget: always submit your answer as a hash, on its own - read the Taking part section of the intro post for more info. At the end of today, this question will be updated with links to further reading on this topic.

entry.title: Re: Issue with custom color theme after kernel update
entry.content:
Hi @tamere ,

without knowing the exact versions involved, I can only guess based on your screenshot. What I see is:

  • In the "not working" case, the shell header is white, not blue. Is this an FLP in the "Spaces & Pages" layout? If so, try updating UI5 to the latest patch of the minor version you're using. The "Spaces & Pages" layout had some teething problems that have been solved in the meantime.
  • In the "not working" case, in your WebGUI transaction, the editable input fields are grey. This was the SAP Horizon design before Theming Base Content 11.6.8, which was delivered with Unified Rendering TCI 2310 (from September 21st, 2023, almost 3 years old). Follow SAP Note 3714784 - Application Issues or Theme Publishing Errors After an S/4HANA System or UI Technology Upgrade to update UR to the latest version and rebuild your theme.

If both don't help, include the information requested in SAP Note 3759487 - Creating a Support Ticket for UI Theme Designer on the ABAP Platform, or create an incident on CA-UI2-THD-S4H so that we can have a deeper look.

Best
Dominik

entry.title: Re: Drop shipment in report configuration of SAP Responsi...
entry.content:
There are multiple questions in your post, let's tackle them 1 by 1

First we will start with the fact that Drop shipment is not currently supported by RDP.

In the case of drop shipment, there's no outbound delivery in the ERP system of the seller, because the delivery is done by the shipping company.

There are statistical GR material documents, which are created in the selling company, when the goods are shipped from the shipping company.

It's also important to note that drop shipments will not be relevant for all scenarios in RDP.

For example, drop shipment import could never be used in Spain Plastic Tax A22 as justification for paid plastic tax for packaging that is subsequently exported by the same company, because the packaging went directly to the customer.

In the case of EU books, where drop shipment could be relevant, RDP uses the material document ID and not the billing document.

Hence the link between the statistical GR material document and the billing document may not be so important.

@Jessy4 wrote:

When creating a report configuration, the company code and delivery document type is mandatory. What is the company code used for exactly? Is it used to filter for products with company code > sales organization > products? Is it used to filter for material/delivery documents? Do I indicate company codes that are involved in shipping (company code in matdocs/deliveries) or selling (company code in invoice)?

The Company Code in the report configuration is used for filtering the logistics data and in some cases filtering the products, for which data is calculated (like you expected company code -> sales organizations -> products which have these sales organizations in the Product Sales table)

The Company Code is also used when creating the pricing conditions.

You put in the filter the company code, which is legally obligated to do the respective report (i.e the one which is registered for plastic tax with the tax agency or with a PRO). RDP is supposed to handle intercompany scenarios (e.g when the selling company is registered, but another company within the same corporation, ships the goods)

entry.title: Колеги з SAP Ukraine спільно з Pickup4Ukraine передали співробітникам SAP у ЗСУ три автомобілі
entry.content:
Колеги з SAP Ukraine спільно з волонтерами Pickup4Ukraine передали мобілізованим співробітникам SAP у ЗСУ та їхнім підрозділам три автомобілі — два позашляховики та пікап.

Значна частина коштів на придбання автомобілів була зібрана завдяки внескам співробітників SAP.

Ініціатива Pickup4Ukraine, заснована колегами SAP у Німеччині, регулярно організовує волонтерські конвої та доставляє транспорт і критично важливі вантажі безпосередньо до підрозділів на основі донатів, що дозволяє швидко реагувати на нагальні запити.

Від початку своєї діяльності Pickup4Ukraine організувала 22 гуманітарні конвої в Україну. Під час найбільшого на сьогодні конвою до Києва команда з 23 волонтерів-драйверів доставила 12 автомобілів. Загалом організація передала українським захисникам 104 транспортні засоби різних типів: пікапи, позашляховики, мінівени, автобуси та квадроцикли.

Нещодавно команда SAP Ukraine зустрілася з Pickup4Ukraine у Києві: у неформальній атмосфері Національного музею народної архітектури та побуту України просто неба команди провели спільний час із прогулянкою та культурною програмою, щоб зміцнити партнерство, узгодити пріоритети та спланувати наступні хвилі допомоги. Такі зустрічі посилюють взаєморозуміння, пришвидшують координацію й підтримують згуртованість нашої спільноти.

Pазом посилюємо оборону та наближаємо перемогу.

Детальніше про ініціативу та допомогу:  SAP Прес-центр
Як долучитися до допомоги: Pickup4Ukraine

entry.title: Re: Setup Alert Monitoring for sap webdispatcher through...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

I regret to inform you that SAP Web Dispatcher is not in the supported scope for the CALM Operations use cases. 

You may refer to the following documents for the Supported components in Cloud ALM and the Health Monitoring use case.

  • Supported components in Cloud ALM : https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/supported-solutions

  • Supported components for Health monitoring : https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/health-monitoring/health-monitoring-setup-configuration.html?anchorId=section_1126136428_c

You may also check this KBA : 3426195 - What SAP Cloud ALM monitoring is available for my SAP Product? to understand how to check for the supported components. 

If a product is listed on the SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Expert Portal Setup Guides page, then SAP Cloud ALM monitoring is available. If the product is not listed, then it is not yet supported. 

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Best practice for scope item 5W2 (Group Valuation) wh...
entry.content:
Hi Sebastian,

You are right, the 5W2 scope item can only be activated during the initial scoping activities. After scoping is saved and complete, no changes to the number of activated ledgers are possible anymore. So the advice would be to activate scope item 5W2 (and thus ledger 4G) during initial scoping if there is a probability that this is needed later on.

Here you find more information on group valuation:
Configuring the Group Valuation Ledger 

SAP Community Blog on Ledger Scoping (see section "... group valuation")  

SAP Knowledge Base Article 3093830 on Group Valuation

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Verena

entry.title: Re: Planned Delivery Cost of Purchasing in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
entry.content:
@Wei_Wum8 Thanks for the Kudos and here is my answer but might need to be validated from your side. There are two migration paths available via the Migration Cockpit ("Migrate Your Data" app):

Option 1 — SIF_PURCH_INF_V2 (Recommended)

"MM - Purchasing info record with conditions"
- Creates the info record header/org/plant data and associated conditions in one object
- Available since CE 1708, status: Released
- This is the equivalent of mass-uploading PPRO — it covers the full info record including condition types like FVA1
(freight/delivery cost)
- Action: Download the Excel template in the Migration Cockpit and verify that FVA1 appears as a valid condition type value in the conditions sub-sheet

Option 2 — SIF_MM_CONDITION

"Condition record for pricing in purchasing (restricted)"
- Creates standalone purchasing condition records (not tied to the info record header)
- Note the word "restricted" — not all condition types are supported; the supported set needs to be verified by downloading the template

entry.title: Re: Displaying Standard Multiple ALV's in a SAP ABAP Report
entry.content:
Introduction:

I got the requirement  from the team member to display two ALV's in a Report. Here I am writing a blog which I tried to implement it in my System.

This is what I wrote in the Modules 
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
***INCLUDE ZORDER_COMBINED_28_STATUS_0O01.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Module STATUS_0100 OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
DATA: go_custom TYPE REF TO cl_gui_custom_container.

MODULE status_0100 OUTPUT.
 SET PF-STATUS 'STANDARD'.
* SET TITLEBAR 'xxx'.

 IF go_custom IS INITIAL.

    CREATE OBJECT go_custom
      EXPORTING
        container_name = 'CC_MAIN'.

    CREATE OBJECT go_splitter
      EXPORTING
        parent  = go_custom
        rows    = 1
        columns = 2.

    CALL METHOD go_splitter->get_container
      EXPORTING
        row    = 1
        column = 1
      RECEIVING
        container = go_cont1.

    CALL METHOD go_splitter->get_container
      EXPORTING
        row    = 1
        column = 2
      RECEIVING
        container = go_cont2.

    CREATE OBJECT go_alv1
      EXPORTING
        i_parent = go_cont1.

    CREATE OBJECT go_alv2
      EXPORTING
        i_parent = go_cont2.

    CALL METHOD go_alv1->set_table_for_first_display
      EXPORTING
        i_structure_name = 'ZORDH_28'
      CHANGING
        it_outtab        = lt_header.

    CALL METHOD go_alv2->set_table_for_first_display
      EXPORTING
        i_structure_name = 'ZORDI_28'
      CHANGING
        it_outtab        = lt_item.

  ENDIF.
ENDMODULE.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Module  USER_COMMAND_0100  INPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE user_command_0100 INPUT.

  CASE sy-ucomm.

    WHEN 'BACK'
      OR 'EXIT'
      OR 'CANC'.

      LEAVE TO SCREEN 0.

  ENDCASE.

ENDMODULE.

 

Final output

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
Hello Prinya,

In my opinion, only those functions that are actually used by you as a customer are relevant. Functions that are not used would therefore not constitute a violation of the usage rights.

This is exactly why it is important to review the SAP Notes associated with the items listed in the Compatibility Check. If a compatibility-scope item appears in the report, the corresponding notes usually help determine whether the functionality is actively used, merely configured, or simply present due to the initial installation.

If the functionality has never been implemented or used, my understanding is that it does not fall within your actual scope of usage and should therefore not be considered a breach of usage rights.

I would appreciate it if other experts could correct me if my understanding is inaccurate.

Best regards,
Herbert

entry.title: Advanced Down Payment Processing Meets Milestone Billing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
If you work in Sales Billing in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you already know that getting money in before you deliver goods or services is good business — and that making sure the system handles that money correctly is also good business (just less glamorous). SAP's Advanced Down Payment Processing framework has been steadily expanding to make both of those things easier. Today, we're excited to introduce the newest addition to that framework: Milestone Billing with Down Payment Requests.

A Quick Recap: What is Advanced Down Payment Processing?
Before we dive into the new process, a little context goes a long way.

Advanced Down Payment (ADP) Processing is SAP's next-generation framework for managing customer down payments end to end — from the initial request, through settlement against customer invoices, all the way to final payment. Over time, it's set to fully replace the "classic" SD down payment approach (as defined in scope item BKJ) with something more powerful, more flexible, and far better suited to complex international business scenarios.

At its core, every ADP process follows the same five-step rhythm:

  1. Request — send a request for down payment to your customer
  2. Receive — record the incoming payment
  3. Deliver — ship goods or render services (for physical goods, receiving the down payment automatically lifts the delivery block)
  4. Invoice & settle — create one or more customer invoices and settle the down payment against them
  5. Receive final payment — collect the remaining balance and complete the transaction

The following graphic provides an overview of these five steps:

What distinguishes the individual ADP processes from each other is how billing is tied to the underlying sales document — and that's where things get interesting.

The Previous Release: Delivery-Related Billing with Down Payments
The first ADP process to ship was Sell from Stock with Down Payment Requests (scope item 7S7). That process is built for the classic logistics scenario: a customer orders standard goods, you request a down payment, the customer pays, the goods are picked and shipped, and you invoice per delivery. In other words, delivery-related billing — the invoice is created with reference to an outbound delivery document.

That model works great for straightforward sell-from-stock transactions. But what about contracts where invoicing follows a schedule, a project milestone, or a phase of work — regardless of whether a delivery has happened yet? For those scenarios, you need order-related billing.

The New Addition: Milestone Billing with Down Payment Requests
Meet Milestone Billing with Down Payment Requests, now available via scope item 7Z1 (Sales Order Processing with Milestone Billing). This process combines order-related milestone billing with the full power of ADP processing.

In this approach, both the down payment request and the settlement invoice are always created with reference to the sales order — not to an outbound delivery. This means billing can proceed on its own schedule, independently of logistics. Whether your team is dealing with a delayed shipment, a multi-phase service contract, or a deal that mixes physical goods with maintenance services, billing doesn't have to wait.

What Kinds of Products Are Supported?
The process supports sales order items of the following item categories:

Item Category

Description

Involves Delivery?

CBAO

Milestone billing plan for trading goods

Yes

CTAX

Milestone billing plan for non-stock products

Yes

CTAD

Milestone billing plan for service products

No

In other words: physical goods, non-stock items, and pure services are all in scope — making this the most versatile billing setup in ADP so far.

Process Flow Overview
The graphic below shows a simple example document flow across sales, delivery management, and financial accounting. It illustrates how all the pieces connect — from sales order creation through final payment.

Let's go through it, step by step:

  1. Sales order with billing plan 
    A sales rep creates a sales order. For each relevant item, they enable down payment requests and set up a billing plan. The plan includes at least one down payment request date (billing type DPR1) and at least one final invoice date. Milestone dates for partial invoices are optional.
  2. Down payment request is created and sent 
    A billing clerk creates the down payment request and posts it to financial accounting. Output is triggered automatically (for example, via email).
  3. Noted item in FI 
    When the down payment request is posted, the system creates a noted item (special G/L indicator F) — a placeholder that signals a down payment is expected without affecting any G/L account balance. The customer is not legally obligated to pay at this point; the request is non-binding.
  4. Incoming payment clears the noted item
    Once the customer pays, an AR accountant clears the noted item. The total amount (net + tax) is posted with special G/L indicator A. For delivery-relevant items (CBAO, CTAX), this can automatically remove the delivery block on the sales order item.
  5. Delivery (D) and partial settlement invoices (if applicable)
    For physical goods, the shipping team creates the outbound delivery, picks, and posts goods issue as usual. If required, a billing clerk creates partial invoices with reference to the milestone billing plan dates. Using the down payment register, the billing clerk can adjust down payment settlement for individual items.
  6. Incoming payment clears partial settlement invoices (if applicable)
    An AR accountant posts incoming payments that the customer has made for any partial invoices.
  7. Final settlement invoice
    A billing clerk creates the final invoice with reference to the final billing plan date. Before posting, they can again use the down payment register to review and flexibly adjust exactly how much of each received down payment is settled against each item. The system reduces the invoice total accordingly.
  8. Final payment 
    The customer pays the remaining open amount (Total Invoice Amount − Settled Down Payment Amount). Once received and posted, the business transaction is complete.

What Makes This Different from the "Classic" Milestone Billing Process with Down Payment Requests?
The previous milestone billing process (scope item BKJ) handled order-related billing but lacked the ADP framework's modern capabilities. Here's what's new:

  • Item-level down payment settlement via the down payment register 
    Instead of a blunt, document-level settlement, you can now allocate and adjust settlement amounts per item, giving you granular control and a clear audit trail.
  • Condition-technique-based settlement logic
    This eliminates rounding differences automatically. 
  • Consistency across Sales and Financial accounting
    The underlying data model keeps Sales and Financial Accounting fully in sync throughout the process, dramatically reducing the risk of inconsistencies.
  • Non-binding down payment requests — The request is legally treated as a non-binding offer. If the customer doesn't pay, you cancel cleanly with no financial impact.

When Should You Use This Process?
This process is a strong fit for scenarios like:

  • Capital equipment sales — selling a factory floor machine with scheduled milestone payments tied to project phases
  • Mixed contracts — the same order includes both the machine (CBAO) and ongoing maintenance service (CTAD)
  • Long-term service engagements — where billing follows predefined phases of work rather than individual shipments
  • Construction and engineering projects — where invoicing on milestones, not deliveries, is contractually required

If your business operates under legal frameworks in Germany, the United States, or other jurisdictions with specific requirements for down payment handling, the process is also designed to help you stay compliant — no custom modifications needed.

Getting Started
To enable this process, activate scope item 7Z1 (Sales Order Processing with Milestone Billing) and complete the required configuration. Full setup guidance is available in the SAP Help Portal under Setting Up Advanced Down Payment Processing.

For a more detailed look at the complete process flow, step-by-step instructions, and related information (billing plans, down payment requests, the down payment register, settlement invoices), see Milestone Billing with Down Payment Requests on SAP Help Portal and scope item 7Z1 in SAP Signavio Process Navigator.

The ADP framework is growing — and with milestone billing now in the picture, it covers both ends of the billing spectrum: delivery-based and order-based. Whether goods are moving out the door or milestones are being crossed on a service contract, advanced down payment processing has you covered.

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content: Herbert is correct.  The WM interface for example is a configuration probably there because you made an initial copy of client 001.  However, if you read the associated SAP note, it gives a clear instruction how to flag these message types as inactive in table T327B.  Please make sure you have the latest ruleset applied as per my video in this blog.

entry.title: ECP Custom Development
entry.content:
Hi,

We are about to start with the process of moving our on-prem ECC system to SAP SuccessFactors EC/ECP. We have been using SAP HR in ECC for about 24 years so as a big company we have lots of custom developments in the payroll space. I am familiar with SuccessFactors most of these custom developments wont be catered for in SuccessFactors. I am curious as to what type of development is allowed in ECP as its running on S/4 and what other companies have done with their custom infotypes and other WebDynpro and Fiori applications? How much custom development can you do on ECP? Did you move your Fiori applications to CAP or RAP applications? Are custom infotypes still allowed? I assume you can build ODATA services? Can you build custom programs for instance if you have a custom posting report, can you use that development on ECP as well? 

Kind Regards

Deon

entry.title: Re: Changing payer field value in the billing document at...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

To achieve the integration of your SAP Customer Checkout PoS with SAP S/4HANA public cloud system, specifically to have the Payer Field in the billing document reflect a custom payment provider, you can consider using custom fields and logic. Here is a step-by-step approach:

Steps to Implement Custom Logic for Payer Field

  1. Create Custom Field:

    • Use the ‘Custom Fields’ app in SAP S/4HANA to create a custom field  that will hold the payment provider information. For more information, see Extensibility for Output Forms and Email Templates of Billing Documents and How to Bring Custom Fields to Billing Document Output,
    • Ensure this field is enabled for the billing document context.

  2. Implement Custom Logic:

    • Use the ‘Custom Logic’ app to implement logic that assigns the custom payment provider to the Payer Field.
    • You might need to explore available BAdIs or enhancement spots that allow modification of billing document fields.

  3. Configure Output Management:

    • Ensure that the custom field is included in the billing document output form template. For more informations, see S/4HANA Cloud - Business partner on Sales documents output 
    • You can use Adobe LiveCycle Designer to modify the form template and bind the custom field.

  4. Testing:

    • Test the integration by creating a sales transaction in SAP Customer Checkout PoS and verify if the billing document in SAP S/4HANA reflects the custom payment provider in the Payer Field.

Additional Considerations

  • If existing BAdIs do not meet your requirements, consider creating a custom enhancement implementation that specifically targets the billing document creation process.
  • Ensure that the custom field is correctly mapped and populated during the transaction process.

This approach leverages SAP’s extensibility features to customize the billing document output according to your business needs.

Please let us know in case you have more questions.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Cloud Identity Services Bug in SAP BTP Trial Environment: Current Status
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We would like to provide an update regarding the current Cloud Identity Services issue in the trial environment.

Some users may encounter a “Forbidden” error after completing the initial setup. This is related to a known bug where the required roles are not correctly assigned during the first-time administrator setup.

During further investigation, our team identified that the issue is more complex than initially expected. As a result, the timeline for the fix needs to be extended. Unfortunately, we cannot provide a confirmed resolution date at this point.

Please be assured that our team is continuing to work on this issue. We will update this community post as soon as there are any relevant changes or once a confirmed timeline becomes available.

We sincerely apologize for the continued inconvenience and thank you for your patience and understanding.
